heading: Protection of certain nuclear facilities and assets from unmanned aircraft
label: 6227 Protection of certain nuclear facilities and assets from unmanned aircraft

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of title 18, the Secretary of Energy may take such actions described in subsection (b)(1) that are necessary to mitigate the threat (as defined by the Secretary of Energy, in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation) that an unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft poses to the safety or security of a covered facility or asset.

children.label: A
children.text: Detect, identify, monitor, and track the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft, without prior consent, including by means of intercept or other access of a wire, oral, or electronic communication used to control the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft.

children.label: B
children.text: Warn the operator of the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft, including by passive or active, and direct or indirect physical, electronic, radio, and electromagnetic means.

children.label: C
children.text: Disrupt control of the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft, without prior consent, including by disabling the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft by intercepting, interfering, or causing interference with wire, oral, electronic, or radio communications used to control the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft.

children.label: D
children.text: Seize or exercise control of the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft.

children.label: E
children.text: Seize or otherwise confiscate the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft.

children.label: F
children.text: Use reasonable force to disable, damage, or destroy the unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft.
The Secretary of Energy shall develop the actions described in paragraph (1) in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Forfeiture
children.label: c .—Forfeiture
children.text: Any unmanned aircraft system or unmanned aircraft described in subsection (a) that is seized by the Secretary of Energy is subject to forfeiture to the United States.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: d .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of Energy and the Secretary of Transportation may prescribe regulations and shall issue guidance in the respective areas of each Secretary to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: identified by the Secretary of Energy for purposes of this section;

children.label: B
children.text: located in the United States (including the territories and possessions of the United States); and

children.label: i
children.text: owned by or contracted to the National Nuclear Security Administration, including any facility that stores or uses special nuclear material; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a national security laboratory or nuclear weapons production facility.

The terms “unmanned aircraft” and “unmanned aircraft system” have the meanings given those terms in section 331 of the FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012 (; note).Public Law 112–9549 U.S.C. 401011See References in Text note below.1
